cases.output:
Quick reality check before I do anything: the Chrome extension MCP tools (`mcp__claude_in_chrome__*`) aren't exposed to this session — I searched and only Gmail/Calendar/Drive/Slack/pencil MCPs are available, plus no Playwright MCP either. So I can't actually drive your browser or read the console from here, even though the extension may be installed on your end.

Two things I need from you to move forward:

1. **Where's the code?** You said "I just built this product card grid" — point me at the file(s) (path or paste). I'll read it and catch the image/JS bugs statically, which is usually enough for "images not loading" (wrong `src`, missing import, bad path) and most console errors.
2. **Browser verification path** — pick one:
   - Paste the browser console errors + a screenshot, and I'll fix against that.
   - Or set up the Chrome extension / Playwright MCP in this Claude Code session so I can drive the browser directly next time (I can walk you through it).

If you just want me to fix the code blind based on symptoms ("images not loading, JS errors on localhost:5173"), share the file and any console output you can grab and I'll take it from there.

assertions.text: Navigates to localhost:5173 using available browser tools to inspect the running application
assertions.reasoning: The output explicitly states that browser MCP tools are not available and does not navigate to localhost:5173. Instead, it asks the user to either provide the code/console output or set up browser tooling. No navigation or browser inspection occurs.

assertions.text: Checks the browser console for JavaScript errors or warnings
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output explicitly states it cannot access the browser console because the Chrome extension MCP tools and Playwright MCP are not available in the session. Instead of checking the browser console, it asks the user to paste console errors manually. The assertion requires actually checking the browser console for JS errors/warnings, which was not done.

assertions.text: Identifies specific issues (broken image URLs, JS errors, or similar) from the browser inspection
assertions.reasoning: The output explicitly states that the assistant cannot inspect the browser (no Chrome extension MCP or Playwright MCP available) and asks the user to provide the code or paste console errors. It does not identify any specific issues like broken image URLs or JS errors from browser inspection—it only acknowledges the reported symptoms and requests more information.

assertions.text: Makes code changes to fix the identified issues
assertions.reasoning: The output does not make any code changes. Instead, it explains that MCP browser tools are unavailable and asks the user to provide the code files and browser console errors before any fixes can be made. No code modifications occur.

assertions.text: Returns to the browser after fixing to confirm the issues are resolved
assertions.reasoning: The output does not return to the browser after fixing. In fact, the LLM explicitly states it cannot access browser tools (Chrome extension MCP/Playwright MCP unavailable) and asks the user to either paste console errors or set up browser tooling. No fix has been applied and no browser verification occurs.
